goal: Expand examples smoke coverage and make gate artifact/status visibility explicit in tracked docs and verification outputs.
successCriteria: Examples smoke covers the current supported examples path and verification artifacts no longer leave gate status/artifact visibility ambiguous.
Tracked docs and verification outputs render gate artifact/status fields without unexplained blanks.
Focused verification proves the new smoke and visibility checks are wired into the DX lane.
touchedSurfaces: verification report or related tracked docs for gate visibility
DX docs describing artifact and gate output semantics
explicitlyOutOfScope: Changing live-verify policy semantics beyond visibility/reporting
Adding new product capabilities outside examples and verification outputs
Rewriting unrelated docs sections already fixed in Wave B
integrationPrerequisites: Wave B is integrated on codex/vnext-integration through commit c3a5d15.
DX package should align with the current verification-report and docs contract rather than redefine policy.

liveVerifyStatus.reason: This package improves smoke coverage and gate visibility rendering; it does not require live credentials.
